title: galium multiflorum var. hirsutum
intro: Galium multiflorum var. hirsutum, also known as the hairy bedstraw, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It is native to parts of Europe and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating wounds. The leaves and stems of Galium multiflorum var. hirsutum are used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
